summary: Broad warning-cleanup pass aimed at eliminating remaining `-Wimplicit-function-declaration` diagnostics, especially across sysdolphin baselib, Dolphin GX/MTX/OS headers, and a few fighter files. The PR promoted many local or missing declarations into headers, corrected include paths/order, moved GX prototypes to more appropriate public headers, added function-pointer typedef usage such as `Event`, and added MWERKS/non-MWERKS guards or stubs around inline assembly. Review feedback emphasized keeping aggregate initializers minimal by relying on C zero-initialization, avoiding unnecessary names for unused parameters, preserving uncertain symbol names unless backed by maps, and retaining some explicit parentheses where they silence clang warnings.
what_changed: Added or corrected prototypes to resolve implicit function declarations, including `__fabsf` in `MetroTRK/intrinsics.h`, `PSMTXMultVecSR` in `dolphin/mtx/mtxvec.h`, GX fog/pixel/viewport/copy functions in GX headers, and baselib declarations such as `hsdIsDescendantOf`, `hsdForgetClassLibrary`, `_HSD_DispForgetMemory`, and `HSD_JObjDisp`.

Renamed `src/dolphin/gx/__GXFrameBuf.h` to `src/dolphin/gx/GXFrameBuf.h` and updated the include guard from `DOLPHIN_GX___GXFRAMEBUF_H` to `DOLPHIN_GX_GXFRAMEBUF_H`; `GXInit.c` now includes the public `GXFrameBuf.h` instead of `__GXFrameBuf.h`.

Moved some GX declarations out of `GX_unknown_001.h` into more specific headers: fog adjustment and pixel format APIs into `GXPixel.h`, texture copy APIs into `GXFrameBuf.h`, and projection/viewport getters into `GXTransform.h`.

Adjusted several function signatures to use project typedefs or discovered parameter lists instead of empty or local prototypes, e.g. animation callbacks in `aobj.c` now use `Event`, `_HSD_AObjForgetMemory` and `_HSD_DispForgetMemory` take `any_t low, any_t high`, and `hsdIsDescendantOf` was exposed as `bool hsdIsDescendantOf(HSD_ClassInfo* info, any_t p)`.
Removed or replaced ad hoc local declarations, such as deleting `bool hsdIsDescendantOf(HSD_ClassInfo* info, HSD_ClassInfo* p);` from `ftparts.c` once the declaration existed in `class.h`.

Added missing includes across baselib C files so callees are declared by headers rather than by implicit declarations, e.g. `aobj.c` includes `MetroTRK/intrinsics.h` and `placeholder.h`, `class.c` includes `string.h` and `hash.h`, `controller.c` includes `OSInterrupt.h`, and `displayfunc.c` includes GX/state/TEV headers.

Wrapped MWERKS-only inline assembly in `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` with non-MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks in files such as `aobj.c` and `__ppc_eabi_init.c`, allowing non-Metrowerks tooling to parse/compile those translation units far enough for diagnostics.

Updated `compile_flags.txt` to keep `-Werror=implicit-function-declaration` while disabling `missing-braces` and `missing-field-initializers` diagnostics (`-Wno-missing-braces`, `-Wno-missing-field-initializers`) instead of treating missing field initializers as errors.

Introduced match-preservation conditionals such as `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` around unused stack bytes, duplicate assignments, forced-active strings, and no-inline pragmas so source can remain warning-clean while retaining matching artifacts when required.
The PR body said everything was done except `mobj`, which the author could not figure out; this is a stated limitation rather than a fully closed warning universe.
smart_moves: Kept the project’s important warning gate, `-Werror=implicit-function-declaration`, and fixed declarations/includes instead of globally disabling the target warning.
Moved declarations into semantically appropriate subsystem headers, reducing dependence on `GX_unknown_001.h` and making future call sites discover the right API through normal includes.
Converted local forward declarations into shared header declarations where call sites span files, which prevents signature drift and makes future warning-cleanup passes easier.
Used existing typedefs such as `Event` and `any_t` to match project conventions and avoid repeated raw function-pointer spellings.
Used `MWERKS_GEKKO` guards to isolate compiler-specific inline assembly from modern tooling, while preserving the original assembly path for matching builds.
Used `MUST_MATCH` blocks to document and contain artificial matching-only constructs instead of leaving them as unexplained warning-prone source.
Left explicit parentheses in `class_set_flags` after review noted they fix a clang warning and aid readability despite being redundant by C precedence.
decomp_lessons: For implicit-function-declaration cleanup, prefer adding the real prototype to the owning header over sprinkling local externs in users; this PR removed at least one local declaration after exposing `hsdIsDescendantOf` in `class.h`.
Header placement matters: public GX APIs should live in named GX headers such as `GXPixel.h`, `GXFrameBuf.h`, and `GXTransform.h` rather than accumulating in an unknown catch-all header.

Do not over-initialize large class-info aggregates purely to satisfy warnings. Reviewer feedback repeatedly said to rely on default zero-initialization for globals like `hsdClass`, `hsdFog`, `hsdJObj`, and `hsdLObj`.
If a parameter is unused, reviewer guidance was to leave it unnamed instead of inventing names such as `arg0`; this avoids implying semantics that are not known.

When preserving weird matching artifacts, guard them with `MUST_MATCH` and add comments such as `@todo Unused stack` or `@todo Duplicate assignment` so later cleanup can distinguish intentional matching hacks from mistakes.

For compiler/tooling compatibility, wrap MWERKS-only asm with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` and provide a placeholder/non-MWERKS body when needed, rather than letting clang-format or clang parsing break on inline assembly.

Be cautious with symbol spelling changes. The `PSMTXMUltiVec`/`PSMTXMultiVec` discussion showed that names seen in other repos or decomp folklore may not be backed by a debug map; avoid changing names without stronger evidence.

Changing compile warning policy can be acceptable when the warning is about benign aggregate initialization style, but the PR preserved the stricter implicit-function-declaration error that was the target of the pass.
naming_conventions: Use public Dolphin GX header names without the internal double-underscore prefix when declaring public-facing functions, exemplified by `GXFrameBuf.h` replacing `__GXFrameBuf.h`.
Header guards mirror public header paths, e.g. `DOLPHIN_GX_GXFRAMEBUF_H` for `src/dolphin/gx/GXFrameBuf.h`.
Prefer project typedefs for callback-like functions: `Event` was used in place of raw `void (*func)()` in animation APIs and `__ctors` handling.
Use `any_t` when the exact pointer type is uncertain or intentionally generic, as in `hsdIsDescendantOf(HSD_ClassInfo* info, any_t p)` and forget-memory range parameters.
Do not invent descriptive parameter names for unused parameters; reviewer explicitly preferred unnamed unused parameters, e.g. `int _hsdClassInit(HSD_Class*)`.

Preserve uncertain original/library spellings unless evidence supports a rename; the `PSMTXMUltiVec` spelling was discussed as possibly historical or copy-pasted rather than verified by Melee debug maps.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Wrapped inline assembly functions such as `__init_hardware`, `__flush_cache`, and `HSD_ForeachAnim` in `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, with non-MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` C stubs to satisfy modern tooling.
Used `SECTION_INIT asm` blocks only on the MWERKS path and kept clang-format control comments around assembly bodies.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` around no-inline pragmas, force-active unused strings, an unused stack buffer in `HSD_AObjLoadDesc`, and a duplicate assignment in `hsdIsDescendantOf`.
Changed `class_set_flags` to `(class_info->head.flags & ~reset) | set`; review called the parentheses redundant semantically but kept them because they fix a clang warning and improve readability.
Added compile flags to suppress missing-brace and missing-field-initializer warnings so short aggregate initializers can remain idiomatic and rely on zero-fill.
review_feedback: r-burns suggested not expanding global aggregate initializers: `HSD_ClassInfo hsdClass = { _hsdClassInfoInit };` is enough because the rest of the object is guaranteed to be zero-initialized.
The same zero-initialization feedback was repeated for `fog.c`, `jobj.c`, and `lobj.c` when class-info globals were expanded into verbose nested zero/null initializers.
r-burns advised that unused parameters should be left unnamed instead of given placeholder names such as `arg0`.

r-burns initially noted `PSMTXMUltiVec` as the actual spelling, then clarified neither spelling was found in a debug map and the spelling may be a decomp-community copy/paste artifact; evidence for renaming was weak.
A proposed removal of parentheses in `class_set_flags` was reconsidered: the parentheses were kept because they silence a clang warning and help readers remember bitwise precedence.

There was a clang-format/inline-asm discussion in `__ppc_eabi_init.c`; the resolution shown in the diff was to wrap MWERKS assembly with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` rather than fight formatter behavior directly.
The PR body noted an unresolved area: everything was done except `mobj`, which the author could not figure out.
Author shared a useful audit command for future passes: `tools/issues.sh | grep '\-Wimplicit-function-declaration'`.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for remaining `mobj` implicit-function-declaration warnings mentioned as unresolved in this PR.
Check whether later work settled the `PSMTXMUltiVec` versus `PSMTXMultiVec` naming question with stronger debug-map evidence.
Audit whether verbose class-info aggregate initializers introduced in this PR were simplified after review feedback or after `-Wno-missing-field-initializers` landed.
Search for follow-up PRs that move more declarations out of `GX_unknown_001.h` into named GX headers.
Search for later cleanup of `MWERKS_GEKKO` and `MUST_MATCH` wrappers added around inline assembly and matching-only artifacts.
Run or inspect `tools/issues.sh | grep '\-Wimplicit-function-declaration'` history around this pass to identify the remaining warning list.
